title: Getting Started with BioAnchor
text: bioanchor lets permanently archive MCMC analysis metadata Arweave permaweb. extracts convergence diagnostics, posterior summaries, model specifications fitted model objects, uploads structured JSON.

title: Getting Started with BioAnchor
code: library(bioanchor) library(posterior) #> This is posterior version 1.7.0 #>  #> Attaching package: 'posterior' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     mad, sd, var #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %in%, match  # Use the built-in eight schools example draws <- example_draws("eight_schools")  # Extract metadata meta <- extract_metadata(draws, model_name = "eight_schools_demo") print(meta) #>  #> ── BioAnchor Metadata ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Model: eight_schools_demo #> ℹ Backend: posterior #> ℹ Timestamp: 2026-05-07T17:06:48+0000 #> ✔ Max Rhat: 1.0235 (converged) #> ℹ Parameters: 10

title: Getting Started with BioAnchor
text: testing (Arweave wallet required):
code: result <- archive_mcmc(draws, model_name = "eight_schools_demo") #>  #> ── BioAnchor: Archiving MCMC Results ── #>  #> ℹ Extracting metadata... #>  #> ── BioAnchor Metadata ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Model: eight_schools_demo #> ℹ Backend: posterior #> ℹ Timestamp: 2026-05-07T17:06:49+0000 #> ✔ Max Rhat: 1.0235 (converged) #> ℹ Parameters: 10 #> ℹ No wallet provided, using mock uploader. #> ℹ [MOCK] Simulated upload (3110 bytes) #> ℹ [MOCK] TX ID: mock_SwlKLUUEdLieexomb92Q9QeuR8HfdVHIyvQgF0fivjF  # Inspect the result result$upload$tx_id #> [1] "mock_SwlKLUUEdLieexomb92Q9QeuR8HfdVHIyvQgF0fivjF" result$upload$payload_size #> [1] 3110

title: Getting Started with BioAnchor
code: meta <- extract_metadata(draws,   model_name = "eight_schools",   extra = list(     author = "Dohoon Kim",     affiliation = "Promptgenix LLC",     project = "Hierarchical model comparison",     doi = "10.5281/zenodo.19709077"   ) )

title: Getting Started with BioAnchor
text: Arweave JWK wallet:
code: result <- archive_mcmc(draws,   model_name = "eight_schools",   jwk_path = "~/.arweave/wallet.json",   extra = list(author = "Your Name") )  # Access the permanent URL browseURL(result$upload$url)

what: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
text: Permanent Archiving MCMC Analysis Metadata Arweave bioanchor bridges Bayesian MCMC analysis R Arweave decentralized storage, enabling permanent, tamper-proof archiving analysis metadata — convergence diagnostics, posterior summaries, model specifications. R companion BioAnchor Python (pip install bioanchor).

title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
code: # From GitHub (development version) remotes::install_github("kdh4win4/bioanchor-r")  # From CRAN (when available) # install.packages("bioanchor")

title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
code: library(bioanchor) library(posterior)  # Fit your model (using any MCMC backend) draws <- example_draws("eight_schools")  # One-step archive (uses mock uploader without a wallet) result <- archive_mcmc(draws, model_name = "eight_schools")  # With a real Arweave wallet result <- archive_mcmc(draws,   model_name = "eight_schools",   jwk_path = "~/.arweave/wallet.json" )  # View the archived metadata print(result$metadata)  # Access the Arweave transaction result$upload$tx_id result$upload$url

title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
code: # Step 1: Extract metadata meta <- extract_metadata(fit, model_name = "my_model",   extra = list(author = "Dohoon Kim", project = "IC50 analysis"))  # Step 2: Validate validate_metadata(meta)  # Step 3: Upload (or mock) result <- upload_to_arweave(meta, jwk_path = "wallet.json") # or: result <- mock_uploader(meta)

title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
text: package uses ar-io.dev default Arweave gateway. legacy arweave.net gateway known unstable. can specify custom gateway:

title: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave
text: BioAnchor (Python) — original Python implementation Arweave — permanent decentralized storage posterior — unified interface MCMC draws R

what: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
title: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
text: convenience wrapper extracts metadata fitted MCMC object uploads Arweave single call.

title: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
code: archive_mcmc(   fit,   model_name = NULL,   jwk_path = NULL,   gateway = "https://ar-io.dev",   tags = NULL,   extra = list(),   dry_run = FALSE )

title: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
text: fit fitted model object (stanfit, brmsfit, mcmc.list, posterior draws). model_name Optional character string naming model. jwk_path Path Arweave JWK wallet file. NULL, uses mock_uploader(). gateway Arweave gateway URL. tags Additional Arweave transaction tags (named character vector). extra Additional metadata include (named list). dry_run TRUE, validate without uploading.

title: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
text: list metadata (bioanchor_metadata object) upload (upload result tx_id url).

title: Archive MCMC Results to Arweave (One-Step Workflow) — archive_mcmc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(posterior) draws <- example_draws("eight_schools")  # Mock upload (no wallet needed) result <- archive_mcmc(draws, model_name = "eight_schools") print(result$metadata)  # Real upload result <- archive_mcmc(draws,   model_name = "eight_schools",   jwk_path = "~/.arweave/wallet.json" ) } # }

title: Internal imports — bioanchor-imports
text: Internal imports

what: bioanchor: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave — bioanchor-package
title: bioanchor: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave — bioanchor-package
text: Bridges Bayesian MCMC analysis R Arweave decentralized storage permanent, tamper-proof archiving analysis metadata. Extracts convergence diagnostics, posterior summaries, model specifications 'Stan', 'brms', 'coda' objects, uploads structured JSON Arweave permaweb.

title: bioanchor: Permanent Archiving of MCMC Analysis Metadata on Arweave — bioanchor-package
text: Maintainer: Dohoon Kim dkim@promptgenix.org (ORCID) Authors: Dohoon Kim dkim@promptgenix.org (ORCID)

what: Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
title: Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
text: Extracts convergence diagnostics, posterior summaries, model specifications various MCMC backends (Stan, brms, coda).

title: Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
text: fit fitted model object. Supported classes: stanfit (rstan) brmsfit (brms) mcmc.list (coda) draws_array, draws_df, draws_list (posterior) model_name Optional character string naming model. extra named list additional metadata include.

title: Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
text: list class "bioanchor_metadata" containing: model_name: character backend: character (e.g., "rstan", "brms", "coda", "posterior") timestamp: ISO 8601 timestamp diagnostics: list Rhat, ESS, divergences (available) summary: posterior summary data frame model_info: model-specific information extra: user-supplied metadata

title: Extract MCMC Metadata from Fitted Model Objects — extract_metadata
code: # With a posterior draws object: library(posterior) #> This is posterior version 1.7.0 #>  #> Attaching package: ‘posterior’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     mad, sd, var #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     %in%, match draws <- example_draws("eight_schools") meta <- extract_metadata(draws, model_name = "eight_schools")

title: Mock Uploader for Testing — mock_uploader
text: drop-replacement upload_to_arweave() simulates upload without requiring wallet network access. Useful testing CI.

title: Mock Uploader for Testing — mock_uploader
text: metadata bioanchor_metadata object (extract_metadata()). gateway Arweave gateway URL. Default uses ar-io.dev (recommended arweave.net reliability). tags named character vector additional Arweave transaction tags.

title: Mock Uploader for Testing — mock_uploader
text: list fake tx_id url.

title: Print method for bioanchor_metadata — print.bioanchor_metadata
text: Print method bioanchor_metadata

title: Print method for bioanchor_metadata — print.bioanchor_metadata
text: x bioanchor_metadata object. ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Print method for bioanchor_metadata — print.bioanchor_metadata
text: x invisibly.

title: Upload Metadata to Arweave — upload_to_arweave
text: Uploads JSON-serialized metadata object Arweave permaweb via standard transaction API.

title: Upload Metadata to Arweave — upload_to_arweave
code: upload_to_arweave(   metadata,   jwk_path,   gateway = "https://ar-io.dev",   tags = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE )

title: Upload Metadata to Arweave — upload_to_arweave
text: metadata bioanchor_metadata object (extract_metadata()). jwk_path Path Arweave JWK wallet file. gateway Arweave gateway URL. Default uses ar-io.dev (recommended arweave.net reliability). tags named character vector additional Arweave transaction tags. dry_run TRUE, serialize validate actually upload.

title: Upload Metadata to Arweave — upload_to_arweave
text: list tx_id (transaction ID) url (gateway URL).

title: Upload Metadata to Arweave — upload_to_arweave
text: Gateway note: ar-io.dev recommended gateway. legacy arweave.net gateway known unstable. Wallet: need Arweave JWK wallet file sufficient AR balance. See https://arweave.org wallet creation.

title: Validate BioAnchor Metadata — validate_metadata
text: Checks metadata object required fields valid diagnostics.

title: Validate BioAnchor Metadata — validate_metadata
text: metadata bioanchor_metadata object.

title: Validate BioAnchor Metadata — validate_metadata
text: TRUE invisibly valid; errors otherwise.
